view_meta: LLM routing, usage & spend, judge dead-man, model config — via litellm-gw01
purpose: MODELS — LLM usage and configuration surface for the TG operator console. Shows (1) role→model routing with fallback chains through the litellm gateway, (2) usage telemetry (cost donut, tokens-by-role hbar, observed-vs-predicted request envelope chart, six metric tiles) across a 24h/7d/30d facet window, (3) the governance judge dead-man liveness status, and (4) per-model config cards with secret-reference API keys and mutation-gated write controls. Epistemic discipline holds throughout: configured/expected values (model ids, temps, envelopes, budgets) render MAP-indigo; measured telemetry (tokens, latency, heartbeats, observed req rate) renders TERRITORY-steel; INV-08 (no model token becomes control flow) is stated as a permanent strip at the top.
notes: VERIFIED: injected into the console-v2 prototype shell (scratchpad console-v2/mdl_test.html) and driven with Playwright — 0 page errors in dark and light themes; nav entry + badge ("2") register; 4 routing rows, 6 tiles, 4 donut segments, 25 heartbeat cells, 4 config cards, 12 gated (disabled) buttons; facet switch to 7d correctly re-derives (requests tile 33,384). Screenshots: console-v2/shots/mdl-dark-{top,mid,bottom,7d}.png, mdl-light-top.png. — DESIGN DECISIONS: (a) The foundation "CHART PRIMITIVES API" section arrived empty in my brief, so the donut/hbar/area charts are module-local renderers built directly on the global svgEl() (with a tiny namespace-correct fallback, mdlSvg, if svgEl is absent); if canonical donut()/hbar()/areaChart() primitives exist, mdlDonut/mdlHbar/mdlReqChart are drop-in swappable call sites. (b) The requests chart is the thesis in miniature: observed req-rate = steel solid over territory-dim fill; the router's own predicted capacity envelope = indigo dashed band; points breaching the envelope get a deviation-red triangle tied to the ledgered gateway-fallback count (also the nav badge). (c) The routing table dual-codes epistemics per column: configured values (temp, model chips) indigo, measured values (reqs, p95) steel — stated in the table's footnote. (d) mdlAddNav wraps the shell's addNav in try/catch with a manual fallback because the prototype's h("svg",{class}) throws on SVGElement.className in strict mode; if the integrated shell's addNav is fixed, mdlAddNav simply delegates to it. (e) The "Secrets ↗" links route to views.secrets only if that module is registered (no-op otherwise). (f) Motion: only two, both genuine liveness — the ticking heartbeat-age counter (reduceMotion-guarded, interval self-clears via isConnected) and the pulsing "next beat due" cell (prefers-reduced-motion media query). (g) Cost figures are internally consistent (per-model costs sum to the window total; per-role request counts sum to the requests tile; judg
